extraction.one_line_summary: LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel soloed to victory from a caught breakaway on the final climb to Calar Alto Observatory, while FROOME Chris distanced key GC rivals.
tactical_patterns: CONTADOR Alberto attacked final 10km attempting stage win despite GC deficit, revealing aggressive mindset when pressured.
Sky isolated leaders early on Pena climb through high pace, fragmenting favorites before final ascent began.
LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel caught BODY Loïc group then dropped them on final ascent, superior climbing on 2000m finish.

rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: positive: At 23, won from caught break on high-altitude finish, confirming 2014 Tour de l'avenir climbing pedigree translates to Grand Tour stages.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Gained time on all GC rivals three days after Cumbres stage win, demonstrating sustained high-altitude dominance.
rider_signals.CHAVES Esteban: negative: Lost 1:50 to Froome and dropped to only 3 seconds ahead of DE LA CRUZ David, fragile podium position.
rider_signals.ROCHE Nicolas: negative: Lost 4 minutes and dropped out of top ten, catastrophic collapse on high-altitude finish.
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: Identified as major loser alongside VAN GARDEREN Tejay, significant time loss undermines early Vuelta ambitions.
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: neutral: Attacked with 10km remaining but failed to bridge, aggressive tactics without power to execute.
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: Caught breakaway with ATAPUMA Darwin on final climb, showing climbing legs but unable to contest stage win.
rider_signals.ATAPUMA Darwin: neutral: Bridged to leaders with YATES Simon but dropped on final ascent, insufficient punch for 2000m finish.
